title: Deployment Environments, OIDC, Workload Identity, and Cloud Authority
description: A deployment uses a long-lived service-account key in repository secrets, trusts any branch subject, grants project-wide admin, bypasses environment review, and leaves credentials reusable after the job. Learners cumulatively produce environment and federated identity policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use named environments for approvals, branch and tag policies, scoped secrets, wait rules, URL evidence, and deployment history.
Request id-token write only in the deployment job and validate issuer, audience, subject, workflow ref, repository identity, event, and environment claims.
Exchange GitHub OIDC identity through a constrained Google Workload Identity Provider and service account without storing a key.
